title: Zosterophyllanthos coriacardia
taxonomy: Zosterophyllanthos coriacardia is a genus of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales. As a member of the family Euphorbiaceae, it is also part of the clade Sapindidae. The classification of Zosterophyllanthos coriacardia reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants. The precise classification of the species is still being refined by botanists and taxonomists.

However, I can provide information on the plant Zostera (seaweed). If you are referring to that, here is a paragraph about it:

 Zostera is a genus of seagrasses, which are marine flowering plants that belong to the family Zosteraceae. The health benefits of Zostera include its potential to improve gut health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Zostera include prebiotic fibers, vitamins, and minerals, as well as bioactive compounds such as phenolic acids and flavonoids. Zostera is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
